findings.headline: Raw user prompts are exposed on the advisor subprocess command line
findings.impact: Raw prompt text remains observable through local process inspection even though persisted hook surfaces stay prompt-safe.
findings.remediation: Send prompt content to skill_advisor.py over stdin or an equivalent non-argv channel and extend privacy tests to cover the subprocess boundary.

findings.headline: Producer and runtime keep two divergent advisor brief formats alive
findings.impact: Stored/shared-payload brief text and runtime-injected brief text can diverge, and observability byte accounting tracks the producer string instead of the actual rendered hook output.
findings.remediation: Adopt one canonical renderer or a structured-fields-only pipeline reused by producer, envelope, and measurement code.

findings.headline: OpenCode plugin defaults to a looser confidence threshold than the core hook stack
findings.impact: The plugin can emit recommendations that the main hook path would suppress, breaking transport parity for the same prompt and workspace.
findings.remediation: Centralize the default threshold constant and add a plugin-vs-hook parity test on the canonical fixture set.

findings.headline: Coverage misses the two highest-signal drift vectors: subprocess privacy and plugin-vs-hook parity
findings.impact: The current suite is broad but would not directly catch the two most valuable regressions identified by this review.
findings.remediation: Add a subprocess command-line privacy assertion and a plugin parity suite that reuses the runtime-parity fixtures.
